name: Mangalyaan
first: First Asian Mars mission; first Mars success on first attempt
description: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission cost $74 million — less than the budget of the film Gravity ($100M), and a fraction of NASA's MAVEN ($671M) which arrived two days earlier. Built in 18 months as a technology demonstrator, Mangalyaan succeeded at every objective: trans-Mars injection, deep-space navigation, Mars orbit insertion, and 8 years of science return. India became the fourth space agency at Mars and the first to succeed on its first try.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: PSLV-XL (C25) from Sriharikota — a relatively small launcher requiring orbit-raising loop.

events.label: PERIGEE BURN 1
events.note: First of seven Earth orbit raising burns.

events.label: TRANS-MARS INJECTION
events.note: Final perigee burn places spacecraft on Mars trajectory.

events.label: MARS ORBIT INSERTION
events.note: Liquid Apogee Motor fires after 4 years dormant — fired for 24 minutes to capture into orbit.

events.label: MISSION END
events.note: Lost contact 2022-04 after spacecraft propellant exhausted.
